delta.text:
Report

**Root cause:** `is_valid()` in `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:60` retur
ned `false` for any `LuaValue::Nil`, including real Nil values sitting on the stack. After `table.sort` called
`lua_settop(L, 2)` to pad the stack with Nil at index 2, `l
ua_type_at(2)` ran `index_to_value` → got `LuaValue::N
il` → `is_valid(&Nil)` returned `false` → reported `LuaType::None`. Then `sort_comp` checked `type_at(2) == Lu
aType::Nil` (which was false because it was `None`), took the user-comparator branch, and tried to call the

Nil slot — hence "attempt to call a nil value".

**Fix:** Replaced value-based `is_valid(&
LuaValue)` with index-based `is_valid_index(state, idx)` that mirrors C's `isvalid(L
, o)` semantics — a real in-range stack slot is valid even if it holds Nil. Updated the three callers (`lua_

type_at`, `raw_equal`, `compare`) to check index validity instead of value validity.

**State after fix:** `local t={3,1,4}
; table.sort(t); print(t[1],t[2],t[3])` now prints `1\t3\t4` (correctly sorted). The original test program
now fails on a *different*, unrelated Phase-B stub (`TODO(port): borrow split needed for io_write`
) which is out of scope for this debug task.

content.text:
## Report

**Root cause:** `is_valid()` in `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:60` returned `false` for any `LuaValue::Nil`, including real Nil values sitting on the stack. After `table.sort` called `lua_settop(L, 2)` to pad the stack with Nil at index 2, `lua_type_at(2)` ran `index_to_value` → got `LuaValue::Nil` → `is_valid(&Nil)` returned `false` → reported `LuaType::None`. Then `sort_comp` checked `type_at(2) == LuaType::Nil` (which was false because it was `None`), took the user-comparator branch, and tried to call the Nil slot — hence "attempt to call a nil value".

**Fix:** Replaced value-based `is_valid(&LuaValue)` with index-based `is_valid_index(state, idx)` that mirrors C's `isvalid(L, o)` semantics — a real in-range stack slot is valid even if it holds Nil. Updated the three callers (`lua_type_at`, `raw_equal`, `compare`) to check index validity instead of value validity.

**State after fix:** `local t={3,1,4}; table.sort(t); print(t[1],t[2],t[3])` now prints `1\t3\t4` (correctly sorted). The original test program now fails on a *different*, unrelated Phase-B stub (`TODO(port): borrow split needed for io_write`) which is out of scope for this debug task.

result:
## Report

**Root cause:** `is_valid()` in `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:60` returned `false` for any `LuaValue::Nil`, including real Nil values sitting on the stack. After `table.sort` called `lua_settop(L, 2)` to pad the stack with Nil at index 2, `lua_type_at(2)` ran `index_to_value` → got `LuaValue::Nil` → `is_valid(&Nil)` returned `false` → reported `LuaType::None`. Then `sort_comp` checked `type_at(2) == LuaType::Nil` (which was false because it was `None`), took the user-comparator branch, and tried to call the Nil slot — hence "attempt to call a nil value".

**Fix:** Replaced value-based `is_valid(&LuaValue)` with index-based `is_valid_index(state, idx)` that mirrors C's `isvalid(L, o)` semantics — a real in-range stack slot is valid even if it holds Nil. Updated the three callers (`lua_type_at`, `raw_equal`, `compare`) to check index validity instead of value validity.

**State after fix:** `local t={3,1,4}; table.sort(t); print(t[1],t[2],t[3])` now prints `1\t3\t4` (correctly sorted). The original test program now fails on a *different*, unrelated Phase-B stub (`TODO(port): borrow split needed for io_write`) which is out of scope for this debug task.
